exemplar.title: Creating an online application for art social networking.

detailedExemplarData.title: Creating an online application for art social networking.

strengths: Comprehensive ATL skills diagram that maps learning strategies to project objectives
Detailed recounting of research skills applied, including primary interviews and secondary source review
Rigorous evaluation of sources via an OPVL table, demonstrating critical thinking
Inâdepth product benchmarking through analysis of Behance.net to inform design decisions
Clear application of selfâmanagement skills with a structured action plan, deadlines, and digital reminder tools
Effective use of survey data and stakeholder interviews to refine userâcentered design
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your application of ATL skills is **exceptionally thorough**. You not only identify the skills you used but provide concrete evidence of their impact on both your learning and the quality of your product. Continuing to document this level of detail will sustain the strong link between skills development and project outcomes.

strengths: Succinct definition of the intended product as an online artânetworking application
Clear statement of the learning goal linked to personal interest in coding and art appreciation
weaknesses: Action plan omits specific resource allocation and leaves the âRelevant Success Criteriaâ column undefined
Success criteria are descriptive but lack measurable targets (for example, defined user engagement metrics or precise color contrast ratios)
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning demonstrates a **strong foundation**: the product is well conceptualized and your learning goal is convincingly tied to your interests in coding and art. To elevate this further, ensure each task in your action plan specifies required resources and that every success criterion is expressed in measurable terms. This will make your plan fully operational and easier to assess.

strengths: Insightful reflection on growth in coding abilities and art appreciation
Wellâstructured table of product changes with clear justifications, showcasing an iterative design mindset
Dataâdriven peer and selfâevaluations of aesthetic criteria, reinforcing valid judgments
Preâ and postâresearch mind maps that vividly illustrate the evolution of your understanding
Thoughtful connection of IB learner profile traits (inquirer, reflector) to your project journey
weaknesses: Linkages between suggested improvements and specific rubric descriptors could be more explicit
Evaluation of the product against each success criterion would benefit from more comprehensive, evidence-based commentary
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflection is **robust and multifaceted**, demonstrating metacognition and a genuine iterative approach. To advance your work, explicitly tie each improvement suggestion back to the rubricâs success criteria and provide detailed evidence for how the final product meets (or falls short of) each one. This will deepen the evaluative rigor of your reflection.

annotations.comment: Your ATLS diagram effectively categorizes learning skills and shows awareness of how each will support your project objectives.
You provide strong layout evaluation data but could strengthen reflection by linking suggested improvements directly to rubric descriptors.
The action plan omits resource allocation and leaves the 'Relevant Success Criteria' column blank; include specific resources and success criteria for each task.
Your intended product is succinctly defined as an online application for art networking, giving clear direction to your planning.
You clearly state your learning goal and link it to personal interest in coding and art appreciation, providing a strong foundation for criterion A planning.
Your success criteria are descriptive but would be more effective if you defined measurable targets (e.g., specific user engagement metrics or color contrast ratios).
You provide a detailed account of using research skills, including primary interviews and secondary sources, clearly demonstrating ATL skills applied to your learning goal.
You demonstrate strong evaluation and selection of information sources, citing scientific and innovative insights that directly inform your application design.
Your detailed product analysis of Behance.net shows clear application of market research to benchmark design features and user experience.
Your OPVL source evaluation table illustrates rigorous critical thinking and source validation in support of your learning goal.
You explain how self-management skills were applied through your action plan and deadlines, showing effective planning and time management.
Your reminder app screenshot concretely evidences the use of digital self-management tools to track objectives and maintain productivity.
You use target-audience survey data to inform design decisions, showing strong application of communication skills to optimize user preferences.
Your interview transcript with an art teacher provides valuable expert insight, illustrating effective communication and stakeholder engagement.
Your reflection on skill development and growth demonstrates deep insight into how the project enhanced your coding and art appreciation abilities.
Your table of product changes with clear justifications shows reflective adaptation and an iterative design mindset.
Your peer and self-evaluation of aesthetic criteria uses data to support judgments, reflecting a robust approach to product evaluation.
You achieve top marks for targeting the audience, supported by unanimous survey results, demonstrating full alignment with your success criteria.
Your pre-research mind map effectively captures baseline understanding and frames your learning journey.
Your post-research mind map crisply illustrates your enhanced insights, showing clear progress in understanding the importance of art.
Your reflection on IB learner profiles shows strong metacognition, linking your inquirer and reflector traits to project outcomes.
